title: Was EoE done with part ink and paint and CAPS or All Caps
display_title: Was EoE done with part ink and paint and CAPS or All Caps
wikitext:
[]: Was EoE done with part ink and paint and CAPS or All Caps             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Apr 03, 2010 9:35 pm Now, I know that Neon Genesis Evangelion was done in ink and paint on regular film, but I couldn't tell in End of Evangelion whether it was done: partially done with ink and paint and CAPS (which is digitally color the animation (not referring to CGI because there is CGI already in EoE) or all done in CAPS animation. (the reason I couldn't tell right away is because the "Renewal" picture looks absolutely crystal clear (compared to NGE) that I couldn't tell the difference.)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Apr 03, 2010 11:03 pm I am fairly certain that FLCL (produced 3 years later) was the first anime produced by Gainax to be digitally inked/colored.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sun Apr 04, 2010 9:58 am So it was partially ink and paint and partially CAPS, then... Fair enough...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Apr 04, 2010 11:05 am Well, it would have to be, EoE incorporates some of the same CGI elements that were in the series that were done by Production IG, namely the computer display animations, like the shots of the magi display being hacked.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Apr 04, 2010 1:10 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I am fairly certain that FLCL (produced 3 years later) was the first anime produced by Gainax to be digitally inked/colored.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Sun Apr 04, 2010 3:04 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Well, it would have to be, EoE incorporates some of the same CGI elements that were in the series that were done by Production IG, namely the computer display animations, like the shots of the magi display being hacked.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Apr 04, 2010 4:49 pm JoeD80 wrote: If we're talking original series episodes, all the CGI in the original is credited to "Gainax Shop". I'm pretty sure they did all the computer displays themselves. Production IG is only credited with key animation on episodes 7 and 13; in-betweening on episodes 7, 9, 13, and 20; and production assistance on 7, 11, and 13.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sun Apr 04, 2010 9:06 pm Episode 13, huh? From the looks of that episode, it's still Ink and paint... Did Production I.G. also do Ink and paint? I thought it would be a pure computerized animation company...

[]:             Quote Post by RMZX » Sun Apr 04, 2010 10:59 pm Wasn't the Asuka vs. the MP Eva fight CGI? It always looked odd compared to the rest of the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Mon Apr 05, 2010 3:38 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Untrue.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Tue Apr 06, 2010 10:08 pm RMZX wrote: Wasn't the Asuka vs. the MP Eva fight CGI? It always looked odd compared to the rest of the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Wed Apr 07, 2010 1:09 pm FYI: CAPS is owned by Disney, not Production I.G.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Wed Apr 07, 2010 6:06 pm Shogo-Kun wrote: FYI: CAPS is owned by Disney, not Production I.G.

[]:             Quote Post by KaaVI » Sat Apr 10, 2010 12:02 pm EDITED DUE TO MISTAKE MY BAD GUYS

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Apr 10, 2010 2:30 pm ...Anyway, after seeing some of the movie again, it's truly indeed partially ink and paint and Computerized. Like I said earlier, the "Renewal" version made it look like it was all animated with computers.

[]:             Quote Post by KaaVI » Sat Apr 10, 2010 8:37 pm tomrule123 wrote: Hey, KaaVI, how is this related to the topic? Next time, stay in TOPIC! ...Anyway, after seeing some of the movie again, it's truly indeed partially ink and paint and Computerized. Like I said earlier, the "Renewal" version made it look like it was all animated with computers.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 06, 2010 2:09 am This may be a minor detail to bump the thread for, but CAPS was a proprietary Disney system that was only ever used by Disney. It doesn't just refer to digitally colored animation; it refers to a specific system that Pixar and Disney put together. It's no longer in use even by Disney; their modern "traditional" animations such as The Princess and the Frog are animated with Toon Boom software. Yamaga said in a 2001 interview that FLCL was the first Gainax animation to be painted digitally and that the first series of Mahoromatic was the last to be painted traditionally.
